title: UPDATE Statement
content:
### UPDATE Statement

The UPDATE statement modifies existing records in a table. Use WHERE to specify which rows to update; omitting WHERE updates ALL rows.

---

### UPDATE Syntax

```sql
UPDATE table_name
SET column1 = value1,
    column2 = value2
WHERE condition;
```

### Basic UPDATE Example

```sql
UPDATE employees
SET salary = 80000
WHERE id = 101;
```

### UPDATE Multiple Columns

```sql
UPDATE employees
SET
    salary = salary * 1.1,
    last_review_date = CURRENT_DATE
WHERE department = 'Engineering';
```

### UPDATE Using a Subquery

```sql
UPDATE products
SET price = price * 1.05
WHERE category_id = (
    SELECT id FROM categories WHERE name = 'Electronics'
);
```

### UPDATE with JOIN

```sql
-- MySQL / PostgreSQL
UPDATE employees e
JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
SET e.bonus = 5000
WHERE d.name = 'Sales' AND e.salary < 60000;

-- SQL Server
UPDATE e
SET e.bonus = 5000
FROM employees e
JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
WHERE d.name = 'Sales' AND e.salary < 60000;
```

### UPDATE All Rows

```sql
-- Updates every row in the table (use with caution!)
UPDATE employees SET active = 1;
```

> **Key Point:** Always include a WHERE clause in UPDATE unless you intend to modify every row. It is good practice to SELECT first to preview which rows will be affected.
codeExample:
UPDATE employees
SET salary = salary * 1.15,
    updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
WHERE performance_rating = 'Exceptional'
  AND salary < 100000;

quiz.question: What happens if you omit the WHERE clause in an UPDATE?
options: All rows in the table are updated
Only the first row is updated

quiz.question: Can UPDATE reference the current column value in its assignment?
